root_text.an5.37:1.1: “Bhojanaṁ, bhikkhave, dadamāno dāyako paṭiggāhakānaṁ pañca ṭhānāni deti.
root_text.an5.37:1.3: Āyuṁ deti, vaṇṇaṁ deti, sukhaṁ deti, balaṁ deti, paṭibhānaṁ deti.
root_text.an5.37:1.4: Āyuṁ kho pana datvā āyussa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā;
root_text.an5.37:1.5: vaṇṇaṁ datvā vaṇṇassa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā;
root_text.an5.37:1.6: sukhaṁ datvā sukhassa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā;
root_text.an5.37:1.7: balaṁ datvā balassa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā;
root_text.an5.37:1.8: paṭibhānaṁ datvā paṭibhānassa bhāgī hoti dibbassa vā mānusassa vā.
root_text.an5.37:1.9: Bhojanaṁ, bhikkhave, dadamāno dāyako paṭiggāhakānaṁ imāni pañca ṭhānāni detīti.

translation_text.an5.37:1.1: “Mendicants, when a giver gives food, they give the recipients five things.
translation_text.an5.37:1.3: Long life, beauty, happiness, strength, and eloquence.
translation_text.an5.37:1.4: Giving long life, they have long life as a god or human.
translation_text.an5.37:1.5: Giving beauty, they have beauty as a god or human.
translation_text.an5.37:1.6: Giving happiness, they have happiness as a god or human.
translation_text.an5.37:1.7: Giving strength, they have strength as a god or human.
translation_text.an5.37:1.8: Giving eloquence, they are eloquent as a god or human.
translation_text.an5.37:1.9: When a giver gives food, they give the recipients five things.
translation_text.an5.37:2.1: Being attentive, one is a giver of life, strength,

variant_text.an5.37:3.2: paṭibhānakaṁ → paṭibhāṇakaṁ (bj); paṭibhānado (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)

reference_text.an5.37:1.1: csp1ed16.36, csp2ed16.36, dr22.46, ms16A5_192, msdiv37, pts-vp-pli3.42
